meta.title: Feat Compendium
meta.note: Official entries are the Ashen-compatible subset normalized from the local 5etools corpus. Setting-exclusive imports are omitted. Ashen Realms entries are the finalized campaign catalogue.

feats.name: A Hundred Graves of Practice
feats.bodyHtml: Your weapon attacks against humanoids score a critical hit on a roll of 19 or 20, and humanoids of CR 1/2 or lower that watch you draw your weapon must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw (DC 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Strength or Charisma modifier) or be frightened of you until you leave their sight.

feats.name: A Room Can Be Read
feats.prerequisite: Level 4, proficiency in Investigation or Perception
feats.abilityIncrease: Increase your Intelligence or Wisdom score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
feats.bodyHtml: After examining an area for 1 minute, ask the DM two questions: which entrance has been used most frequently; what was disturbed most recently; which object was deliberately placed out of position; where someone most likely waited or observed from; or which exit was used most recently by a Small or larger creature. The answers reflect surviving physical evidence, not magic, identities, or erased traces. Once per short or long rest.

feats.name: A Rumor of Where
feats.bodyHtml: Once per short rest, when you leave every creature's line of sight, your position becomes a rumor: you may next appear in any unoccupied space within 30 feet of where you were last seen, without having crossed the distance between.
feats.searchText: a rumor of where ashen realms sideways dexterity 15+, level 8 once per short rest, when you leave every creature's line of sight, your position becomes a rumor: you may next appear in any unoccupied space within 30 feet of where you were last seen, without having crossed the distance between.

feats.name: A Thought You Can Hand Over
feats.prerequisite: Level 4, Intelligence 13+, proficiency in an Intelligence skill; Cerebrax origin or training
feats.abilityIncrease: Increase your Intelligence score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
feats.bodyHtml: After a long rest, crystallize a number of thought-shards equal to your proficiency bonus. Assign each shard an Intelligence skill in which you are proficient. A creature holding a shard may crush it before making a check with its assigned skill, replacing its Intelligence modifier and proficiency bonus with your current Intelligence modifier and proficiency bonus. Expertise and other bonuses are not transferred, and only one shard can affect a check. Unused shards dissolve at your next long rest.

feats.name: A Wound Is a Road
feats.bodyHtml: As a bonus action, choose a living, blood-bearing creature you can see within 60 feet that is below its hit point maximum and an unoccupied space you can see within 5 feet of it. You take 1d8 + your proficiency bonus damage, which cannot be reduced or prevented, then teleport to the chosen space if you remain conscious. You may use this feat a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus per long rest.

feats.abilityIncrease: Increase Strength, Dexterity, Constitution, Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma by 2 (maximum 20).
feats.bodyHtml:

Repeatable

feats.name: Actor
feats.abilityIncrease: Charisma by 1 (maximum 20).
feats.bodyHtml:
Skilled at mimicry and dramatics, you gain the following benefits:

  • You have advantage on Charisma (Deception) and Charisma (Performance) checks when trying to pass yourself off as a different person.
  • You can mimic the speech of another person or the sounds made by other creatures. You must have heard the person speaking, or heard the creature make the sound, for at least 1 minute. A successful Wisdom (Insight) check contested by your Charisma (Deception) check allows a listener to determine that the effect is faked.

feats.name: Actor
feats.abilityIncrease: Charisma by 1 (maximum 20).
feats.bodyHtml:
You gain the following benefits.

Impersonation

While you're disguised as a real or fictional person, you have Advantage on Charisma (Deception or Performance) checks to convince others that you are that person.

Mimicry

You can mimic the sounds of other creatures, including speech. A creature that hears the mimicry must succeed on a Wisdom (Insight) check to determine the effect is faked (DC 8 plus your Charisma modifier and Proficiency).

feats.name: Alert
feats.bodyHtml:
Always on the lookout for danger, you gain the following benefits:

  • You gain a +5 bonus to initiative.
  • You can't be surprised while you are conscious.
  • Other creatures don't gain advantage on attack rolls against you as a result of being unseen by you.

feats.name: Alert
feats.bodyHtml:
You gain the following benefits.

Initiative Proficiency

When you roll Initiative, you can add your Proficiency to the roll.

Initiative Swap

Immediately after you roll Initiative, you can swap your Initiative with the Initiative of one willing ally in the same combat. You can't make this swap if you or the ally has the Incapacitated condition.

feats.name: An Honest Meal
feats.bodyHtml: When you hit a creature with your bite, you may drink deep: it takes an extra 2d6 necrotic damage and you regain hit points equal to the necrotic damage dealt. You may use this trait a number of times equal to half your proficiency bonus (rounded up) per long rest.

feats.name: Artificer Initiate
feats.bodyHtml:
You've learned some of an artificer's inventiveness:

  • You learn one cantrip of your choice from the artificer spell list, and you learn one 1st-level spell of your choice from that list. Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for these spells.
  • You can cast this feat's 1st-level spell without a spell slot, and you must finish a long rest before you can cast it in this way again. You can also cast the spell using any spell slots you have.
  • You gain proficiency with one type of artisan's tools of your choice, and you can use that type of tool as a spellcasting focus for any spell you cast that uses Intelligence as its spellcasting ability.

feats.name: Athlete
feats.abilityIncrease: Increase Strength or Dexterity by 1 (maximum 20).
feats.bodyHtml:
You have undergone extensive physical training to gain the following benefits:

  • When you are prone, standing up uses only 5 feet of your movement.
  • Climbing doesn't cost you extra movement.
  • You can make a running long jump or a running high jump after moving only 5 feet on foot, rather than 10 feet.

feats.name: Blood for Life, Exactly
feats.bodyHtml: At the end of a short or long rest, after Hit Dice are recovered, another willing creature may give you blood by expending one Hit Die without receiving healing. Until that creature finishes its next long rest, the first time it would be reduced to 0 hit points without being killed outright, it drops to 1 hit point instead. You then take necrotic damage equal to twice your level, which cannot be reduced or prevented, even if it reduces you to 0 hit points. The covenant functions only while you are alive and on the same plane. A creature can belong to only one such covenant, and forming a new covenant ends your previous one. Blood obtained through coercion cannot establish it.

feats.name: Bloodlust
feats.abilityIncrease: Increase Strength, Dexterity, or Constitution by 1 (maximum 20).
feats.bodyHtml:
You gain the following benefits.

Powerful Recovery

When you roll a Hit Point Dice to regain Hit Points, you can treat any roll of 1 or 2 as a 3.

Sanguine Feast

Once per turn when you hit a Bloodied creature that isn't a Construct or Undead with an attack roll, you can expend a Hit Point Dice, roll it, and regain a number of Hit Points equal to the number rolled plus your Constitution modifier.

You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Proficiency, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

feats.name: Boon of Defiant Law
feats.abilityIncrease: Increase one ability score by 1, to a maximum of 30.
feats.bodyHtml:
Local Law. As a bonus action, impose one of the following laws in a 30-foot Emanation originating from you for 1 minute:

  • No creature can teleport, be banished, or travel to another plane.
  • Invisible creatures gain no benefit from being Invisible.
  • No creature can regain hit points or gain temporary hit points.
  • No creature can be moved or knocked Prone against its will.

The law applies to every creature in the Emanation, including you, and the Emanation moves with you. You may dismiss it without using an action. Once you impose a law, you cannot do so again until you finish a short or long rest.

feats.name: Boon of Fluid Forms
feats.abilityIncrease: Increase Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma by 1 (maximum 30).
feats.bodyHtml:
You gain the following benefits.

Shapechanger

You can take a Magic action to shape-shift into a Beast, Humanoid, or Monstrosity with a Challenge Rating no higher than 10. When you shape-shift, you gain a number of Temporary Hit Points equal to the Hit Points of the form. The shape-shifting effect lasts for 1 hour, and it ends early if you have no Temporary Hit Points left or if you take a Magic action to return to your true form.

Your game statistics are replaced by the stat block of the chosen form, but you retain your creature type; alignment; personality; Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma scores; Hit Points; Hit Point Dice; proficiencies; and ability to communicate. If you have the Spellcasting or Pact Magic feature, you retain it too. Upon shape-shifting, you determine whether your equipment drops to the ground or changes in size and shape to fit the new form while you're in it.

Once you use this benefit, you can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest.

Hardy Transformation

When you gain Temporary Hit Points when you shape-shift, increase that number of Temporary Hit Points by 20.

feats.bodyHtml: No Claim on You. You are immune to the Grappled, Paralyzed, Petrified, and Restrained conditions. Your speed and physical form cannot be altered against your will, and you cannot be moved, teleported, banished, or sent to another plane against your will.

feats.name: Boon of the Unbroken Company
feats.abilityIncrease: Increase one ability score by 1, to a maximum of 30.
feats.bodyHtml: Many Hands, One Turn. Whenever you finish a long rest, name up to your proficiency bonus willing creatures. Once on each of your turns, you may forgo your action, your bonus action, or any amount of your movement. One conscious named creature within 60 feet that can see or hear you may use its reaction to immediately take the same kind of action or bonus action, or move up to the distance you surrendered. A creature can receive only one part of another creature's turn each round.

feats.name: Boon of the Unfinished Grave
feats.abilityIncrease: Increase one ability score by 1, to a maximum of 30.
feats.bodyHtml: Prepare the Vacancy. During a long rest, you may spend 1 hour binding yourself to a grave-sized space you touch. You can have only one waiting grave; binding another ends the previous bond. The Grave Has Not Finished. If you die on the same plane as your waiting grave and your soul is free and willing, you return to life beside it at the next dawn with all your hit points. Your body reforms there if your remains were destroyed after your death, bringing any objects you were wearing or carrying when you died that remain with those remains. This benefit fails if the effect that killed you transformed or destroyed your body or soul, caused you to rise as an Undead, or stated that you cannot return to life. When you return, you gain 2 levels of Exhaustion, the grave loses its power, and you cannot bind another until you finish seven long rests.

feats.name: Boon of the Weighted Coin
feats.abilityIncrease: Increase one ability score by 1, to a maximum of 30.
feats.bodyHtml: The Other Face. When a creature you can see within 60 feet makes a d20 roll, you may use your reaction after seeing the number rolled but before the outcome is declared. Replace the number on the d20 with 21 - the number rolled; a 20 becomes a 1, a 19 becomes a 2, and so on. Modifiers are then applied normally. The replacement is the roll's natural result and cannot itself be rerolled or replaced. Once you use this reaction, you cannot use it again until the start of your next turn.

feats.name: Bountiful Luck
feats.bodyHtml:
Your people have extraordinary luck, which you have learned to mystically lend to your companions when you see them falter. You're not sure how you do it; you just wish it, and it happens. Surely a sign of fortune's favor!

When an ally you can see within 30 feet of you rolls a 1 on the d20 for an attack roll, an ability check, or a saving throw, you can use your reaction to let the ally reroll the die. The ally must use the new roll.

When you use this ability, you can't use your Lucky racial trait before the end of your next turn.

feats.name: Bring the Country With You
feats.prerequisite: Level 8; you have experienced a Sovereign regional or environmental effect
feats.bodyHtml:
When you succeed on a saving throw against a regional or environmental effect, you may capture one consequence that the effect could have imposed: one damage type, forced movement, or the blinded, charmed, deafened, frightened, poisoned, prone, or restrained condition. You may carry only one consequence and capture one per long rest.

Before your next long rest, release it as an action in a 15-foot cone. Your feat save DC is 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Constitution modifier. Creatures of your choice in the cone suffer one of the following effects:

  • Damage. A Dexterity save; 4d8 damage of the captured type on a failure, or half on a success.
  • Movement. A Strength save; pushed 20 feet directly away from you on a failure.
  • Condition. The same kind of save required by the original effect; the captured condition lasts until the end of your next turn on a failure. You count as the source of a captured charmed or frightened condition. A captured prone result simply knocks the target prone.

The consequence disappears after release or at your next long rest.

feats.name: Cartomancer
feats.prerequisite: Level 4, Spellcasting or Pact Magic feature
feats.bodyHtml:
You have learned to channel your magic through a deck of cards. You can use a card deck as your spellcasting focus, and you gain the following benefits:

  • Card Tricks You learn the Prestidigitation cantrip and can use it to create illusions that duplicate the effects of stage magic. When you use Prestidigitation in this way, you can conceal the verbal and somatic components of the spell as ordinary conversation and card handling.
  • Hidden Ace When you finish a long rest, you can choose one spell from your class's spell list and imbue that spell into a card. The chosen spell must have a casting time of 1 action, and it must be a level for which you have spell slots. The card remains imbued with this spell for 8 hours. While the card is imbued with the spell, you can use a bonus action to flourish the card and cast the spell within. The card then immediately loses its magic.

feats.bodyHtml:
Thanks to extensive practice with the crossbow, you gain the following benefits:

  • You ignore the loading quality of crossbows with which you are proficient.
  • Being within 5 feet of a hostile creature doesn't impose disadvantage on your ranged attack rolls.

feats.name: Crossbow Expert
feats.abilityIncrease: Dexterity by 1 (maximum 20).
feats.bodyHtml:
You gain the following benefits.

Ignore Loading

You ignore the LD property of the Hand Crossbow, Heavy Crossbow, and Light Crossbow (all called crossbows elsewhere in this feat). If you're holding one of them, you can load a piece of ammunition into it even if you lack a free hand.

Firing in Melee

Being within 5 feet of an enemy doesn't impose Disadvantage on your attack rolls with crossbows.

feats.name: Ritual Caster
feats.bodyHtml:
You have learned a number of spells that you can cast as rituals. These spells are written in a ritual book, which you must have in hand while casting one of them.

When you choose this feat, you acquire a ritual book holding two 1st-level spells of your choice. Choose one of the following classes: bard, cleric, druid, sorcerer, warlock, or wizard. You must choose your spells from that class's spell list, and the spells you choose must have the ritual tag. The class you choose also determines your spellcasting ability for these spells: Charisma for bard, sorcerer, or warlock; Wisdom for cleric or druid; or Intelligence for wizard.

If you come across a spell in written form, such as a magical spell scroll or a wizard's spellbook, you might be able to add it to your ritual book. The spell must be on the spell list for the class you chose, the spell's level can be no higher than half your level (rounded up), and it must have the ritual tag. The process of copying the spell into your ritual book takes 2 hours per level of the spell, and costs 50 gp per level. The cost represents material components you expend as you experiment with the spell to master it, as well as the fine inks you need to record it.

feats.name: Strike of the Giants
feats.sourceName: Bigby Presents: Glory of the Giants

feats.bodyHtml:
You have absorbed primeval magic that gives you an echo of the might of giants. When you take this feat, choose one of the benefits listed below. Once per turn, when you hit a target with a melee weapon attack or a ranged weapon attack using a thrown weapon, you can imbue the attack with an additional effect depending on the benefit you chose:

Cloud Strike

The target takes an extra 1d4 thunder damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw, or you become invisible to it until the start of your next turn or until immediately after you make an attack roll or cast a spell.

Fire Strike

The target takes an extra 1d10 fire damage.

Frost Strike

The target takes an extra 1d6 cold damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a Constitution saving throw, or its speed is reduced to 0 until the start of your next turn.

Hill Strike

The target takes an extra 1d6 damage of the weapon's type. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a Strength saving throw or have the prone condition.

Stone Strike

The target takes an extra 1d6 force damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be pushed 10 feet from you in a straight line.

Storm Strike

The target takes an extra 1d6 lightning damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a Constitution saving throw, or it has disadvantage on attack rolls until the start of your next turn.

The saving throw DC for these effects equals 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Strength or Constitution modifier.

You can use this feat a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

feats.name: This Wall Is Younger Than I Am
feats.bodyHtml: As an action, choose a manufactured or magically altered physical wall, door, gate, or portcullis created after the Usurpation. Walls made wholly of force and worn or carried objects are ineligible. Until the end of your turn, a section up to 10 feet square and 5 feet thick becomes transparent and non-solid to you and one willing creature touching you. That creature may immediately use its reaction to move up to its speed through the section without provoking Opportunity Attacks. Neither creature can end inside the barrier. If the barrier is ineligible or has no legal exit, the use is not expended. Once per long rest.

feats.name: Treacherous Allure
feats.abilityIncrease: Increase Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma by 1 (maximum 20).
feats.bodyHtml:
You gain the following benefits.

Enchanting Presence

You always have the Charm Person spell prepared. You can cast it without a spell slot, and you must finish a Long Rest before you can cast it in this way again. You can also cast it using spell slots you have of the appropriate level. Your spellcasting ability for the spell is the ability increased by this feat.

Inevitable Betrayal

You have Advantage on attack rolls against creatures with the Charmed condition.

feats.name: Vampire Hunter
feats.bodyHtml:
You gain the following benefits.

Adroit Escape

You have Advantage on checks to escape from nonmagical restraints or the Grappled condition.

Vitality Ward

When you take Necrotic damage, you can take a Reaction to mitigate the damage. Roll a number of d6s equal to your Proficiency, and add them together. Reduce the Necrotic damage you take by this total. Once you use this benefit, you can't use it again until you finish a Short Rest or Long Rest.

feats.name: Vampire Touched
feats.abilityIncrease: Increase Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma by 1 (maximum 20).
feats.bodyHtml:
You gain the following benefits.

Vampire Magic

Choose one level 1 spell from the Enchantment or Illusion school of magic. You always have that spell and the Spider Climb spell prepared. You can cast each of these spells without expending a spell slot, but when you cast Spider Climb this way, you must target yourself, and you must finish a Long Rest before you can cast each spell in this way again. You can also cast either spell using spell slots you have of the appropriate level. Your spellcasting ability for the spells is the ability increased by this feat.

feats.name: Vampire's Plaything
feats.bodyHtml:
You gain the following benefits.

Decanting

When you finish a Long Rest, you can create one Potion of Healing or an Antitoxin, as long as you have an empty vial or flask. These liquids evaporate when you finish another Long Rest.

Timely Retreat

You can take a Bonus Action to take the Dash action or the Disengage action. You can use this benefit a number of times equal to your Proficiency, and you recover all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

Vampiric Connection

The DM determines the fate of your former vampire master. While you and your former vampire master are on the same plane of existence, the vampire can communicate with you telepathically, and you can choose to allow the vampire to perceive through your senses.

feats.name: Venom-Render
feats.bodyHtml: You keep a renderer's vial as part of your poisoner's kit. When you or a creature within 5 feet takes poison damage, you may use your reaction to roll a number of d6s equal to your proficiency bonus, reducing the damage by the total rolled. Store the amount actually prevented in the vial. Alternatively, you may spend 10 minutes harvesting a creature that died within the past hour and possessed a poison attack, storing poison damage equal to twice your proficiency bonus. The vial can hold only one amount, which remains until your next long rest. As a bonus action, apply the stored venom to one weapon or piece of ammunition. Its next hit within 1 minute deals the stored amount as additional poison damage, then the vial empties.

feats.name: War Caster
feats.bodyHtml:
You have practiced casting spells in the midst of combat, learning techniques that grant you the following benefits:

  • You have advantage on Constitution saving throws that you make to maintain your concentration on a spell when you take damage.
  • You can perform the somatic components of spells even when you have weapons or a shield in one or both hands.
  • When a hostile creature's movement provokes an opportunity attack from you, you can use your reaction to cast a spell at the creature, rather than making an opportunity attack. The spell must have a casting time of 1 action and must target only that creature.

feats.bodyHtml:

Reactive Spell

feats.name: Yield My Flesh to Claim Their Bones
feats.prerequisite: Level 8; proficiency with a martial melee weapon
feats.bodyHtml:
When a creature within your melee reach declares an attack against you, you may use your reaction before its attack roll. The attack automatically hits. You cannot reduce, prevent, redirect, or defer its damage, or voluntarily use a feature to avoid an additional effect it imposes; immunities still apply.

After the attack resolves, if you remain conscious and are not incapacitated, make one melee weapon attack or Unarmed Strike against the attacker with advantage. On a hit, it deals additional damage equal to the damage dealt by the triggering attack. Once per short or long rest.

feats.name: Your Footing Is Mine
feats.bodyHtml: When you hit a creature no more than one size larger than you with a melee weapon attack, you may force it to make a Strength saving throw against a DC equal to 8 + your proficiency bonus + the ability modifier used for the attack. On a failure, you and the target exchange spaces. Neither movement provokes Opportunity Attacks, and the exchange fails if either creature cannot legally occupy the other's space. You may use this feat a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus per long rest.
